reasoning: The question asks if the number of 'Battles' in Ukraine over a 30-day period will exceed 10 times the baseline 30-day average from the preceding year. The baseline 30-day average is approximately 2,005 battles (yielding a target threshold of >20,062 battles in a 30-day period). Recent data from mid-2025 indicates that monthly battles reached an all-time high of around 2,600. Reaching over 20,000 battles in a single month would require an unprecedented, physically and logistically near-impossible level of clashes, or a massive methodological error/redefinition by ACLED (which would be extremely rare and likely corrected). Therefore, the probability of exceeding this 10x threshold is virtually zero across all time horizons. I am applying the minimum allowable probability of 0.05 for early dates, slightly increasing to 0.07 for the furthest date to account for any unforeseen tail risks as time horizons extend.
